questions.question: Explain the difference between functional and non-functional security requirements with examples.
expectedPoints: Functional requirements specify security functions (e.g., authentication mechanism)
Non-functional requirements specify security qualities (e.g., system availability under attack)
Provide specific examples of each type

questions.question: Describe the security requirements engineering process from asset identification to requirement validation, including the techniques used at each stage.
expectedPoints: Threat identification using techniques like STRIDE
Requirement elicitation using abuse cases, workshops, etc.
Validation through reviews and traceability matrices
